Sounds chill but Day 3 would start with whatever is most important to you British Museum gets absolutely rammed so have a plan of what you want to see beforehand

Day 2 Walking from the Tower to Westminster is doable and it takes around 4060 minutes to do so still if I were you I would take a bus to have a bit of rest between those locations you will walk a lot anyway The views are also nice I would say more diverse than if you go along the river

I would take the uber boat from the Tower of London to Westminster Its a lovely part of the river to be on and you see london in a different way Then you can explore the Westminster area A nice loop from the pier is 1 Houses of Parliament and Big Ben 2 Westminster Abbey 3 St Jamess Park 4 Buckingham Palace 5 Down The Mall to Trafalgar Square 6 Portrait Gallery optional if you have time but its free and they have toilets and a nice cafe 7 Down Whitehall passing Downing Street Horseguards and the Cenotaph 8 back to the start

Suggestionafter Tower of London go across Tower Bridge turn rightwalk along the Southbankso much to see can go through Bourough Market for a snack pass Tate Modern and Globe theater walk under the Eye great photos and amazing photos of Westminister and Elizabeth TowerBig Ben from this vantage pointcross Westminister Bridge for a closer look about of Parliament and Westministerthere is a sculpture garden you will see also of important people in history Neals Yard is cool quaint but a been and done in minutesgreat photo momentacross from it is Seven Dials marketgreat for a bite to eat lovely shops in the area and theaters I dont think its too much We love London and easily did 2530000 steps a day

You can move Neils Yard and Covent Garden to day 2 late afternoonevening With the right route you could get Cecil Court in before the shops shut note some shut very early if you were actually planning on going inside If just a walk down the outside then anytime is fine obviously Day 3 Again Do Hampstead later afternoonevening unless you actually plan on clothes shopping The Heath doesnt shut so you can do a later afternoon walk or early evening walk All the pubs and restaurants in Hampstead are open late Or if youre an early bird you can do a Health walk at something like 6630am pop to a cafe in South End Green or Hampstead Village for breakfast and then head to the British Museum

Generally this sounds like a really fun itinerary Some things to be aware of The stalls at Camden Market are shut in the evening but its a buzzy area with food bars etc Fun alternative vibes still despite losing some of its famous countercultural feel Some of the street food places are Insta famous so are a little expensive for what they are Day 3 probably too much here You could probably visit the Hunterian tour only I think or the British Museum Both might feel rushed Would potentially recommend choosing a part of the British Museum that particularly interests you and focusing on it rather than trying to take all of it in this makes things less memorable and the crowds can really be a lot Both Hunterian and British Museum are about 15mins walk from Cecil CourtNeils Yard so would be good places for a browse afterwards

seems fine Remember that Camden Market is not open for stalls and shops in the evening there will be some drinks places open though Maybe do this on sunday If you are adventurous you can walk to the stadium or a bus
If you are into pubs there are some great ones in Hampstead I highly recommend the Old White Bear on New End Square and the King william VI on the high street

I think its a great plan Try to keep outside or in daylight for as long you can It really helps with jetlag As others have said Camden market isnt an evening thing but you can walk the canals and neighbourhoods I wouldnt plan dinner but when you see a pub with outdoor seating assuming weather is nice pop in and check the menu You really wont go far wrong and may have a real adventure

Id also add in Tate modern Portrait gallery Brought market Notting Hill market I prefer this more to Camden Market Maritime museum Hyde parkRichmond Park Richmond park has wild deers Kew gardens Im a Londoner and Ive never been to Tower museum

Looks like a good plan to me You could consider a morning stroll around Hampstead on the day of the match as they are both in North London Pretty easy journey from Hampstead Heath to Upper Holloway on the Overground And then on your British Museum day that gives you more time for another big ticket item in central Also if youre doing the classic Tower Bridge to Westminster walk on the Southbank anyway you might as well pop your head into Borough Market whilst youre there Yeah its crowded but loads and loads of genuinely banging food
I second this plus it depends on how much time you wish to allocate to the British Museum I could easly spend the whole day in there
Sounds good We are going on a Friday when they are open late so hoping that will help Alternative could be to do tower tour in evening and do British when it opens
The walk along the South Bank is one of the most iconic walks in London I definitely wouldnt skip it
I would take the Uber boat from St Katherines Dock to Embankment
A second vote for Seven Dials market for lunch Its across the street from Neals Yard Which is a five minute look and photo Lots of fun shopping the area
Hawley Wharf market which is also by the canal in Camden opens until 8pm
Hawley Wharf food market which opened a few years ago across the road from the traditional Camden stalls is open until 8pm Its also by the canal and has a nice vibe
Clocks go fwd on Sunday so will be lighter evenings

Columbia road is not a bad shout though They can take the Windrush line Overground from Hoxton to Highbury right after 15min journey to get to the match Broadway Market is also not far so they could potentially do a loop around this pocket of east London
British Museum is huuuugeyou can easily spend 35 hours there and not see it allfirst thing in the morning is a mustall sorts of lines

Keep to early for Tower as planned as you skip the Crown Jewels queue Switch Day 3 around so you do BM in morning Its busy but when I went around 10am it certainly wasnt rammed as there is plenty of space in there
Thames Clippers with marketing money from uber departs from Tower Pier not St Katherine Dock

Hawley wharf is an open air shopping mall totally out of place in camden mostly fast food and fast fashion type stuff But a sandwich on the canal is great if weather is nice Can walk up to coal drops along canal to kings cross
My bad youre absolutely correct

Hawley Wharf is not a shopping mall Its an indoor market mostly devoted to food like so many that have popped up all over London offering a chance to try different cuisines and the good thing is people dont have to agree because everyone can order from a different stall These types of market are nothing special but theyre also nice and easy choices Agree that Coal Drops Yard is another good choice
